economic_notes: Cote Market Development Inc. (wholly-owned, modified equity method) reported total revenue of $3,307,293 and net loss of $4,943. PHC Holdings Limited Partnership investment at cost (14.28%). Duck Mountain Resorts Limited Partnership and Duck Mountain Resorts Ltd. consolidated. COVID-19 impacted operations in March 2020. Claims against Government of Canada being pursued (1903 Railway, 1904 Kamsack, Road Allowances, Misadministration claims). RBC loans secured by insurance policies with AmTrust Europe Limited covering claim loan repayments.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 statements signed May 9, 2023 - significant delay in audit completion. Auditor issued Emphasis of Matter paragraph regarding claims against Government of Canada and associated RBC loans covered by insurance. Consolidated entities include Cote First Nation, Cote First Nation CMHC Housing, Cote Resources Ltd., Duck Mountain Resorts Limited Partnership, and Duck Mountain Resorts Ltd. (Dec 31 year-end adjusted for significant events). Cote Market Development Inc. accounted for using modified equity method. Cote Onakawek Trust and Cote Legacy Trust excluded from consolidation as controlled by independent trustees. Large deferred revenue balance of $6,974,144 primarily from Remediation Project ISC ($3,617,137) and Cote Legacy Trust per capita ($682,861). Capital lease obligations for John Deere equipment. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + FNIHB + CMHC + Government of Saskatchewan + PHAC = $7,815,811 + $2,241,913 + $508,203 + $1,000,145 + $278,625 = $11,844,697; OSR includes Cote Legacy Trust, First Nations Trust, YTC, Onakawek Trust, lease, rental, other, CDC, interest, donations, investment loss.
